features.summary: Goldman Sachs reported solid second quarter results for 2024, with net revenues of $12.7 billion and net earnings of $3 billion, resulting in an earnings per share of $8.62. The firm achieved a return on equity (ROE) of 10.9% for the quarter and 12.8% for the first half of the year, driven by strong performance in global banking, markets, and asset wealth management. The firm maintained its leading position in M&A and equity underwriting, with a significant increase in its investment banking backlog.
features.management_outlook: Management expressed optimism about the ongoing recovery in capital markets and M&A activity, noting that they are in the early stages of this resurgence. They anticipate exceeding $50 billion in alternatives fundraising for the year, supported by a strong pipeline and record assets under supervision. The firm is also focused on leveraging advancements in artificial intelligence to enhance productivity and client service, which they believe will drive future growth.
features.risks: The operating environment remains challenging due to geopolitical instability and persistent inflation, which could impact client activity and market conditions. Additionally, the firm is navigating increased capital requirements from regulatory stress tests, which may affect its capital management strategies and ability to repurchase shares in the future.
features.key_drivers: Key drivers of performance included a 14% year-over-year increase in global banking and markets revenues, with notable growth in advisory and underwriting activities. Asset wealth management revenues rose 27% year-over-year, bolstered by record management fees and private banking revenues. The firm also reported record financing revenues across both fixed income and equities, reflecting strong client demand and effective capital deployment.
